key_insight: Nitrogen reduction activity in intermetallic catalysts is governed not just by the d-band center, but also strongly by s- and p-band electronic descriptors and local atomic charges.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a compact, DFT-trained ML descriptor set that can accelerate screening of non-noble intermetallic catalysts for sustainable ammonia synthesis.
method: Built a high-throughput DFT dataset for 47 bimetallic intermetallic compounds across surface sites.
Trained ML models using intrinsic material properties, band centers/fillings, and neighboring-atom Bader charges.
Used SHAP analysis to identify the most influential adsorption descriptors for N2, N2H, and NH3.
evidence_signal: The dataset contains about 1,200 adsorption-energy data points, and the best ML models achieved MAEs of 0.26 eV for N2, 0.39 eV for N2H, and 0.17 eV for NH3 with only 20 features.
watch_item: The descriptor ranking is derived from a limited set of 47 IMCs, so broader transferability to other catalyst families and surface chemistries should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Going Beyond the d-band Center to Design Intermetallic Catalysts for Nitrogen Reduction: A High-Throughput DFT and Machine Learning Study
